source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 13.2-13.3.1 interfaces and ip (pp. 237-239)

options: The loopback - it lets TCP/IP programs on the machine talk to each other and lets you ping localhost
A logical bridge to the router
questions.why: lo is the loopback interface; packets sent to localhost come back identically, useful for testing the network stack.

questions.q: On Rocky 8/9/10, which service assigns interface names like enoX/ensX/enpXsX?
questions.why: udev (integrated into systemd) names interfaces by type/location: enoX on-board, ensX PCIe hotplug slot, enpXsX physical location.

questions.q: Which older command has the ip command replaced?
options: ifconfig (no longer installed by default on Rocky 8/9/10)
questions.why: ip (from iproute2) replaces the historical ifconfig, which is not installed by default anymore.

questions.q: Display the IP address information for the interface eth0.
questions.why: ip addr show <iface> shows that interface's addresses; ip addr show alone covers all interfaces.
ip neigh shows the ARP/neighbour table.
